channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian investors maintain support for green investment taxonomy despite global energy turbulence
item.description: Institutional investors in Canada remain committed to developing a national guidebook for certifying green and transition investments, even as conflict in the Middle East destabilizes global energy ma...

item.title: Labour Signals Potential Welfare Reforms, Facing Possible Backbench Opposition
item.description: Labour’s Education Secretary, Bridget Phillipson, has indicated the possibility of further welfare reforms, potentially sparking renewed conflict with Labour backbenchers. This follows a recent Govern...

item.title: Late Spring Snow Brings Much-Needed Moisture to Edmonton
item.description: On April 7, 2026, Edmonton woke up to yet another spring snowfall, extending an already prolonged winter. Two months earlier, Alberta’s mascot Balzac Billy correctly predicted more winter weeks — and ...

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith begins detention at Silverwater Correctional Complex
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, the former Special Air Service Regiment corporal and ex-Seven executive, has been placed in Silverwater Correctional Complex following his arrest at Sydney Airport. Charged with fiv...

item.title: Adani Group challenges US fraud case, claims lack of jurisdiction
item.description: Gautam Adani, the chairperson of Adani Group, has filed a petition in a US court seeking the dismissal of a fraud case filed against him and his nephew, Sagar Adani, by the US Securities and Exchange ...

item.title: The Importance of Usable Revenue Systems for Modern Publishers
item.description: In the current digital publishing landscape, the traditional model of producing strong content to attract loyal readers is increasingly insufficient. Audience attention is fragmented across multiple d...

item.title: South African Players Shine in Early IPL 2026 Action
item.description: The Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 is already showcasing the talent of several South African cricketers. Heinrich Klaasen, despite his Sunrisers Hyderabad team's mixed start, is proving a valuable a...

item.title: Why ash brown hair colour may not be as flattering as it seems
item.description: Ash brown, a smoky brunette shade with cool, greyish undertones, has become a popular choice among celebrities like Dua Lipa and Hailey Bieber. It’s now the most requested hair colour in salons, but b...

item.title: Blue Jays fall 4-1 to Dodgers despite scoring chances
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays struggled to convert scoring opportunities in their 4-1 loss to the Los Angeles Dodgers on Tuesday. Despite loading the bases with no outs in the eighth inning, Toronto could not...

item.title: US lawmakers react to Trump’s ceasefire with Iran, revealing deep partisan divides
item.description: President Donald Trump's announcement of a temporary ceasefire with Iran on April 7, 2026, has sparked sharply divided reactions among US lawmakers, underscoring the growing rifts over America's forei...

item.title: Kenya’s transformation must reach every region and community
item.description: The article argues that Kenya’s development agenda should not be measured only by progress in a few major urban centres. Instead, real transformation must be inclusive and connect every part of the co...

item.title: Seven West's Coverage of Ben Roberts-Smith's Arrest
item.description: This week, the shocking news of Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest at Sydney Airport made headlines. The former soldier, a decorated war veteran, was taken off a Qantas flight from Brisbane in handcuffs and c...

item.description: Arsenal's dramatic last-minute victory over Sporting CP in the Champions League quarter-finals has not only boosted their own chances but also given a significant advantage to their Premier League riv...

item.title: Reflections on Trump, Phil Ochs, and Lost Ideals of America
item.description: In his op-ed ‘Voices of America’, Mahir Ali draws poignant parallels between the turbulent politics of Donald Trump’s presidency and the dissenting voices of past American icons like Phil Ochs and Pau...

item.title: Vice President Highlights IGNOU’s Role in Expanding Accessible Education Across India
item.description: At the 39th Convocation of Indira Gandhi National Open University (IGNOU) held in New Delhi on April 7, 2026, Vice President C.P. Radhakrishnan praised the institution as a cornerstone of India’s open...

item.title: Trump Announces Ceasefire Deal with Iran to Reopen Strait of Hormuz for Two Weeks
item.description: Donald Trump has claimed a 'total and complete victory' after reaching a two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran to reopen the strategically important Strait of Hormuz. Both the US and Iran have framed...

item.title: Government proposes expanded control over online news amid declining TV viewership
item.description: Amid rising concerns over sensationalism in Indian news channels regarding West Asia, the Union government directed the Broadcast Audience Research Council to suspend Television Rating Points publicat...

item.title: Pakistan’s Mediation Helps US and Iran Agree on Two-Week Ceasefire
item.description: Washington ne Pakistan ki chhupi hui diplomacy ko highlight kar raha hai jo US aur Iran ke darmiyan two-week ceasefire tak le gayi. February 28 ke baad conflict shuru hone par Islamabad ne turant acti...

item.title: U.S. Pledges Support to Stabilize Strait of Hormuz Shipping Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: In April 2026, President Donald Trump announced that the United States will assist in addressing maritime traffic issues in the Strait of Hormuz, a strategic waterway crucial to global oil shipping. H...

item.title: DP Kindiki shifts strategy in Mt Kenya ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki is changing his political approach in Mt Kenya as the 2027 elections draw near. Once known for sharp exchanges with rivals, particularly Rigathi Gachagua of the Democr...
